message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-vqodq (laplacian_matrix dtype parity)

Fixed in commit 56447e9. laplacian_matrix now mirrors nx's
sp.sparse.dia_array construction so D inherits A's dtype: int64
for unweighted/integer-weighted graphs, float64 only when the
input has float weights. Also silences the recent scipy
FutureWarning about diags_array(..., dtype=float).

Sweep: 6642 passed, 0 failed (+3 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -133,7 +133,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-v9m7b","title":"parity: triadic_census dict iterates in arbitrary Rust order, nx uses canonical 16-triad-type order","description":"fnx.triadic_census returns a dict with the 16 triad types in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['120U', '300', '012', '111D', ...]). nx returns them in the canonical MAN-notation order ['003', '012', '102', '021D', '021U', '021C', '111D', '111U', '030T', '030C', '201', '120D', '120U', '120C', '210', '300']. Drop-in code that iterates 'for triad_type, count in census.items(): ...' (or saves the dict in a JSON/CSV in canonical order) breaks. Fix: reorder result via the existing _TRIAD_TYPES constant which is already in canonical order.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:23:30.454400037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:25:34.743757311Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:25:34.741701377Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 028ea58. triadic_census now returns dict in canonical MAN-notation order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ve04b","title":"parity: join_trees signature was (T1, T2, root1, root2) — nx is (rooted_trees, *, label_attribute, first_label)","description":"fnx had join_trees(T1, T2, root1=None, root2=None) producing tuple-labelled nodes (('T1', 0), etc). nx's modern API takes a list of (tree, root) pairs and produces an integer-labelled tree under a new synthetic root. Drop-in callers writing for nx hit TypeError. Rewrote join_trees to nx's literal algorithm (relabel via convert_node_labels_to_integers, attach to first_label root, optional label_attribute). Verified isomorphism with nx on balanced_tree(2,4)+balanced_tree(2,4) -> balanced_tree(2,5), exact edge equality, label_attribute preservation, first_label offsetting, empty input. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:03:11.470408243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:03:58.996024397Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:03:58.994073173Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 99c70f5. join_trees rewritten to match nx's modern API (rooted_trees, *, label_attribute, first_label). 7 parity tests freeze the contract incl. exact edge equality with nx and the balanced_tree(2,h) docstring example.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-vevfq","title":"parity: greedy_color dict iteration order is Rust-internal, nx uses processing-order from strategy","description":"fnx.greedy_color returns dict keys in arbitrary Rust internal order. nx returns them in the order the strategy processed them (e.g. for 'largest_first': sorted by degree descending then in some tiebreak). Drop-in code that iterates 'for node, color in greedy_color(G).items(): ...' breaks. Same iteration-order family. Fix: delegate to nx so the ordering is preserved exactly (Rust impl is for performance only; values match either way).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:59:18.087860408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:02:04.463238548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:02:04.460926228Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit b9b495e. greedy_color now delegates to nx for processing-order match. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-vqodq","title":"laplacian_matrix returns float64 instead of int64 for unweighted graphs","description":"fnx.laplacian_matrix returns a float64 sparse array even when the graph is unweighted (no edge weights). nx returns int64 in that case (matches the int dtype of nx.to_scipy_sparse_array on a weight-less graph).

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> fnx.laplacian_matrix(g).toarray().dtype
  dtype('float64')
  >>> nx.laplacian_matrix(nx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3)])).toarray().dtype
  dtype('int64')

The values agree element-wise, but downstream code that sizes buffers based on dtype, exports to formats with int constraints (e.g., GraphBLAS bool/int variants), or just uses np.array_equal across libraries gets a divergence.

Root cause: python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:12674 forces dtype=float on the diags_array call:
    D = scipy.sparse.diags_array(
        np.asarray(A.sum(axis=1)).flatten(), dtype=float
    )

nx (lib/python/networkx/linalg/laplacianmatrix.py) uses dia_array without forcing dtype, so D inherits A's dtype.

Fix: drop the dtype= kwarg so D's dtype is inferred from the input vector, which itself derives from the sparse-array sum (same dtype as A).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:04:12.504904522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:04:43.003710122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-vqodq","title":"laplacian_matrix returns float64 instead of int64 for unweighted graphs","description":"fnx.laplacian_matrix returns a float64 sparse array even when the graph is unweighted (no edge weights). nx returns int64 in that case (matches the int dtype of nx.to_scipy_sparse_array on a weight-less graph).

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3)])
  >>> fnx.laplacian_matrix(g).toarray().dtype
  dtype('float64')
  >>> nx.laplacian_matrix(nx.Graph([(1,2),(2,3)])).toarray().dtype
  dtype('int64')

The values agree element-wise, but downstream code that sizes buffers based on dtype, exports to formats with int constraints (e.g., GraphBLAS bool/int variants), or just uses np.array_equal across libraries gets a divergence.

Root cause: python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:12674 forces dtype=float on the diags_array call:
    D = scipy.sparse.diags_array(
        np.asarray(A.sum(axis=1)).flatten(), dtype=float
    )

nx (lib/python/networkx/linalg/laplacianmatrix.py) uses dia_array without forcing dtype, so D inherits A's dtype.

Fix: drop the dtype= kwarg so D's dtype is inferred from the input vector, which itself derives from the sparse-array sum (same dtype as A).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:04:12.504904522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:09:39.386147794Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T05:09:39.383175176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-vw7p2","title":"parity: connectivity is_locally_k_edge_connected missing","description":"networkx.algorithms.connectivity.is_locally_k_edge_connected(G, s, t, k) tests local k-edge-connectivity between s and t. Not exposed by franken_networkx. Add Python wrapper delegating to networkx and export at top-level.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:51:45.929293966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:15:26.852652308Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:15:26.850843424Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in fnx — top-level export matches nx.connectivity exactly. Landed tests/python/test_connectivity_parity_extras.py (commit 3765516) with 37 cases freezing the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-wcdm3","title":"parity: Graph add_*/remove_* methods reject nx-canonical kwarg names (u_of_edge, node_for_adding, ebunch_to_add, n, nodes, ebunch)","description":"All four Graph classes use fnx-internal positional arg names that differ from nx's documented kwarg names. Drop-in callers using the documented kwarg form hit TypeError: G.add_edge(u_of_edge=0, v_of_edge=1), G.add_node(node_for_adding=0), G.add_weighted_edges_from(ebunch_to_add=...), G.remove_node(n=0), G.remove_nodes_from(nodes=...), G.remove_edges_from(ebunch=...). MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph add_edge takes u_for_edge/v_for_edge per nx. Fix: thin Python wrappers that accept the nx-canonical names and forward to the rust impl. Add parity tests asserting both kwarg-form calls work and inspect.signature names match nx.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:18:44.567118639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:27:45.419544506Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:27:45.417701899Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f748b59. All Graph class wrappers now use nx's documented kwarg names (u_of_edge/v_of_edge for simple add_edge, u_for_edge/v_for_edge for multi add_edge, node_for_adding, ebunch_to_add, n, nodes, ebunch). Drop-in callers using the documented kwarg form now work on fnx exactly like nx. Also added _wraps_without_signature_poisoning helper so to_directed/to_undirected error messages match nx's wording (Graph.to_undirected vs <locals>.to_undirected). 32 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-wjv9o","title":"parity: tree.branchings + tree.mst edge iterators (5 fns)","description":"Five tree-related utilities from networkx.algorithms.tree not surfaced. Branchings: branching_weight(G, attr=weight, default=1), minimal_branching(G, *, attr=weight, default=1, preserve_attrs=False, partition=None). MST edge iterators: boruvka_mst_edges, kruskal_mst_edges, prim_mst_edges (all signature G, minimum, weight=weight, keys=True, data=True, ignore_nan=False, partition optional). These give users access to the underlying MST/branching algorithm primitives. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:24:47.743148985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:39:41.161615863Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:39:41.159730985Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 7cca063: branching_weight + minimal_branching + boruvka/kruskal/prim mst_edges iterators. All 5 verified on weighted K5 (MSTs match nx exactly) and 3-node DiGraph (branching weight + edge count match).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
